revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=山室姫塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=千葉県山武郡松尾町山室914-2 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|scale:20000}} |形状=円墳 |規模=墳丘径64～66m |築造年代=7世紀 |出土品= |史跡指定=1992年 千葉県指定 |埋葬施設= }} '''山室姫塚古墳'''（やまむろひめづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町山室にある[[古墳]]。[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|千葉県の史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == [[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]中流西岸標高33メートルの[[台地]]上に立地し、他に16基の古墳があり大塚古墳群と呼ばれる。本古墳は台地先端に位置する大塚古墳群の1号墳であり、墳丘径64～66メートル、高さ9メートルを測る千葉県では最大の[[円墳]]である。木戸川上流東岸に位置する[[小池大塚古墳|小池]]・[[芝山古墳群]]や、下流東岸に位置する[[大堤権現塚古墳|大堤]]・[[蕪木古墳群]]が[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[古墳群]]であるのに対し、この大塚古墳群はすべて円墳で構成されており、本古墳と墳丘径29メートルの2号墳以外は径20メートル前後の小型円墳である。
[[発掘調査]]は実施されていないが、測量から周溝の幅は5～9メートルとされ、また周溝の外縁に沿って周堤が認められ、周堤を含めた古墳の全長は約85メートルと捉えられている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[7世紀]]代の築造と考えられているが、同じく7世紀代の築造と考えられる[[作田川]]沿いの[[駄ノ塚古墳]]が[[方墳]]であるのに対し、異なる形式を採ることについても検討課題とされている。
== 参考文献 == * 『続日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、2002年。ISBN 4-490-10599-1
== 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.99coast.jp/yamahime_sanbu_kofun.html 九十九里地域13市町村観光情報 山武市山室姫塚古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまむろひめつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=05|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|35|40|05|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''山室姫塚古墳'''（やまむろひめづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町山室にある[[古墳]]。千葉県の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|author=千葉県教育庁教育振興部文化財課指定文化財班|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=山室姫塚寺古墳|work=千葉県|accessdate=2020-05-18}}。 == 概要 == [[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]中流西岸標高33メートルの[[台地]]上に立地し、他に16基の古墳があり大塚古墳群と呼ばれる。本古墳は台地先端に位置する大塚古墳群の1号墳であり、墳丘径64～66メートル、高さ9メートルを測る千葉県では最大の[[円墳]]である。木戸川上流東岸に位置する[[小池大塚古墳|小池]]・[[芝山古墳群]]や、下流東岸に位置する[[大堤権現塚古墳|大堤]]・[[蕪木古墳群]]が[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[古墳群]]であるのに対し、この大塚古墳群はすべて円墳で構成されており、本古墳と墳丘径29メートルの2号墳以外は径20メートル前後の小型円墳である。 [[発掘調査]]は実施されていないが、測量から周溝の幅は5～9メートルとされ、また周溝の外縁に沿って周堤が認められ、周堤を含めた古墳の全長は約85メートルと捉えられている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[7世紀]]代の築造と考えられているが、同じく7世紀代の築造と考えられる[[作田川]]沿いの[[駄ノ塚古墳]]が[[方墳]]であるのに対し、異なる形式を採ることについても検討課題とされている。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=大塚|first=初重|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=小林|first2=三郎|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|title=続日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|year=2002|date=2002|pages=24-25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://webtv.jiu.ac.jp/99/yamahime_sanbu_kofun.html 九十九里地域13市町村観光情報 山武市山室姫塚古墳] {{DEFAULTSORT:やまむろひめつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]] [[Category:円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

''Yamamuro Himezuka kofun'' || [[Sanmu]] || || || {{c ... 42 KB (4,875 words) - 18:00, 10 January 2025 [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Japanese burial mounds |Name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |Image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |Location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[category:most recent 500]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Japanese burial mounds |Name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |Image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |Location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[category:most recent 500]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sanmu]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} *
