{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{{{Infobox ancient site" to "{{Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 burial mounds. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round burial mounds. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square burial mound, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List
