{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Artifacts Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |Construction Period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun |Group = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |Historical Site Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |Burial Facility = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Cultural Properties Section, Designated Cultural Properties Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designated as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation Designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods = |other_designation = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in form compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_other_designation Section, Designated other_other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō
