Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_other_designation Section, Designated other_other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |other_designation designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been other_designation1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, other_designation Section, Designated other_designation Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=山室姫塚古墳 |所属=大塚古墳群 |画像=[[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|山室姫塚古墳（千葉県山武市松尾町山室）]] |所在地=千葉県山武市松尾町山室914-2 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |形状=円墳 |規模=墳丘径64～66m |築造年代=7世紀 |出土品= |史跡指定=1992年（平成4年）千葉県指定 |埋葬施設= |地図2={{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=山室姫塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=05|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|05|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''山室姫塚古墳'''（やまむろひめづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町山室にある[[古墳]]。千葉県の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=千葉県教育庁教育振興部文化財課指定文化財班|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=山室姫塚寺古墳|work=千葉県|accessdate=2020-05-18}}。 == 概要 == [[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]中流西岸標高33メートルの[[台地]]上に立地し、他に16基の古墳があり大塚古墳群と呼ばれる。本古墳は台地先端に位置する大塚古墳群の1号墳であり、墳丘径64～66メートル、高さ9メートルを測る千葉県では最大の[[円墳]]である。木戸川上流東岸に位置する[[小池大塚古墳|小池]]・[[芝山古墳群]]や、下流東岸に位置する[[大堤権現塚古墳|大堤]]・[[蕪木古墳群]]が[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[古墳群]]であるのに対し、この大塚古墳群はすべて円墳で構成されており、本古墳と墳丘径29メートルの2号墳以外は径20メートル前後の小型円墳である。 [[発掘調査]]は実施されていないが、測量から周溝の幅は5～9メートルとされ、また周溝の外縁に沿って周堤が認められ、周堤を含めた古墳の全長は約85メートルと捉えられている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[7世紀]]代の築造と考えられているが、同じく7世紀代の築造と考えられる[[作田川]]沿いの[[駄ノ塚古墳]]が[[方墳]]であるのに対し、異なる形式を採ることについても検討課題とされている。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=大塚|first=初重|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=小林|first2=三郎|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|title=続日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|year=2002|date=2002|pages=24-25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html 山室姫塚古墳] 山武市教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまむろひめつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]] [[Category:円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=05|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|05|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''山室姫塚古墳'''（やまむろひめづかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武市]]松尾町山室にある[[古墳]]。千葉県の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=千葉県教育庁教育振興部文化財課指定文化財班|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=山室姫塚寺古墳|work=千葉県|accessdate=2020-05-18}}。 == 概要 == [[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]中流西岸標高33メートルの[[台地]]上に立地し、他に16基の古墳があり大塚古墳群と呼ばれる。本古墳は台地先端に位置する大塚古墳群の1号墳であり、墳丘径64～66メートル、高さ9メートルを測る千葉県では最大の[[円墳]]である。木戸川上流東岸に位置する[[小池大塚古墳|小池]]・[[芝山古墳群]]や、下流東岸に位置する[[大堤権現塚古墳|大堤]]・[[蕪木古墳群]]が[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[古墳群]]であるのに対し、この大塚古墳群はすべて円墳で構成されており、本古墳と墳丘径29メートルの2号墳以外は径20メートル前後の小型円墳である。 [[発掘調査]]は実施されていないが、測量から周溝の幅は5～9メートルとされ、また周溝の外縁に沿って周堤が認められ、周堤を含めた古墳の全長は約85メートルと捉えられている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[7世紀]]代の築造と考えられているが、同じく7世紀代の築造と考えられる[[作田川]]沿いの[[駄ノ塚古墳]]が[[方墳]]であるのに対し、異なる形式を採ることについても検討課題とされている。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=大塚|first=初重|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=小林|first2=三郎|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|title=続日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|year=2002|date=2002|pages=24-25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html 山室姫塚古墳] 山武市教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまむろひめつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]] [[Category:円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
Bot: remove incorrect Shona (sn:) interwiki links
Removing from [[Category:Pages without english wikidata name]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11467730]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|qq=|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11467730]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11467730]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] -
