[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11467730]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and the mound's construction in the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} have been noted. [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11467730]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun | part_of = Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster |image = [[File:Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture) 05.jpg|280px|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun (Yamamuro, Matsuo-cho, Sanmu City, Chiba Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Matsuo-machi, Yamamuro 914-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.668056|N|140.420278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound diameter: 64–66 meters |built = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |burial goods Found = |Historic Site designation1 = Designated by Chiba Prefecture in 1992 |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamamuro Himezuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=13|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|5|N|140|25|13|E}}}} }} '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' (山室姫塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Matsuo-machi Yamamuro area. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Chiba Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|author=Chiba Prefecture Education Promotion Division, Historic Site Section, Designated Historic Site Team|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-078.html|title=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|work=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-18}}. == Overview == The Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is situated on a plateau 33 meters above sea level on the western bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kido River (Chiba)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518415}}, which flows through the center of the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}. It is part of the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster, which consists of 16 Kofuns. Positioned at the tip of the plateau, the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun is the first mound in the cluster and measures 64–66 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height, making it the largest {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Chiba Prefecture. In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns. Apart from the Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun and the second mound, which has a diameter of 29 meters, most of the mounds are smaller, measuring around 20 meters in diameter. Although no excavation has been conducted, surveys indicate that the surrounding trench has a width of 5–9 meters. A surrounding embankment follows the outer edge of the trench, giving the mound a total length of approximately 85 meters, including the embankment. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and the mound's construction in the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} have been noted. However, the difference in type compared to the nearby {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the Sakutagawa River, which was also built in the 7th century as a square Kofun, remains a topic for further study. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|Japanese|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|pages=24–25|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001478.html Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun] - Sanmu City Board of Education {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山室姫塚古墳|version=101256206|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山室姫塚古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%AE%A4%E5%A7%AB%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11467730}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sanmu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern bank, or the {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|lt=Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster is composed entirely of round Kofuns.
